The Court's tentative ruling is vacated and the matter is off calender per the email request of the moving party submitted on February 27, 2019.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Set For Trial On Apr-15-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Flora Vigo, Esq. 415-894-9284 of Emergent LLP for Plaintiff Andrea Mangan.
Robert Weiss, Esq. 415-829-4221 of the Mitzel Group for Defendant GILLES DESAULNIERS, CALIFORNIA HARVEST COMMISSARY, INC., CALIFORNIA HARVEST HEALTHY FOODS RANCH MARKET, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT GILLES DESAULNIERS, CALIFORNIA HARVEST COMMISSARY, INC., CALIFORNIA HARVEST HEALTHY FOODS RANCH MARKET, INC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S VERIFIED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Gilles Desaulniers, California Harvest Commissary, Inc. and California Harvest Healthy Foods Ranch Market, Inc's demurrer to the first, second and third causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Andrea Mangan and Carole Morton is overruled as to all three causes of action. The dissolution claims are adequately alleged. Per Corporations Code 1800(a) and (b)(4), Mr. Desaulniers' shares are excluded when determining whether Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton have a sufficient interest for standing because they accuse him of persistent unfairness and fraud. Once Mr. Desaulniers' shares are excluded, Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton satisfy the one-third requirement. Paragraphs 37 and 40 of the complaint allege that Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton are excluding Mr. Desaulniers' interest in reaching their calculation and there is no inconsistency. Mr. Desaulniers' reply argument that the fraud or persistent mismanagement is not alleged with the requisite particularity lacks merit. Bauer v. Bauer (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1106 is not a pleading case and the cases discussing the pleading requirements for a common law fraud claim are inapposite. Paragraphs 12-28 sufficiently allege "persistent unfairness" to support the dissolution claims. Paragraph 15 alleges "misconduct through at least 2015" and paragraph 18 refers to mismanagement and "behavior increasingly erratic well into 2017." The breach of fiduciary duty claim is not facially time-barred. Paragraphs 12-27 allege misconduct through 2017 and the continuous accrual doctrine applies. Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton sufficiently allege bad faith conduct outside of the business judgment rule.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
